questions.text: Which of these turns blue litmus red?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus paper red. Bases turn red litmus paper blue.
Sodium hydroxide (NaOH) is a strong base commonly used in soap making and drain cleaners.

questions.correctAnswer: A reaction between an acid and a base to form salt and water.
questions.explanation: Neutralization is the reaction where an acid reacts with a base to produce salt and water. Example: HCl + NaOH → NaCl + H₂O

questions.text: The pH of a neutral solution is ________.
questions.explanation: A neutral solution has pH = 7. Acids have pH < 7, bases have pH > 7.
